title: Training is badly documented
author: jl2035
body:
With documentation at multiple places (sometime contradictory) it's impossible to figure out the correct steps to train. What I've gathered so far is:

1. Create datasource using camera and csi data
2. Combine into a single file (paired)
3. Train
4. Somehow convert to .rvf

So I try to follow this procedure. First I execute these two simultaniously:
```
python scripts/collect-ground-truth.py --duration 300 --output data/session5 --camera 0 --server http://127.0.0.1:3000
python scripts/record-csi-udp.py --duration 300 --output data/session5
```

Combine:
`node scripts/align-ground-truth.js --gt data/session5/keypoints_*.jsonl --csi data/session5/csi-*.csi.jsonl --output data/session5/paired.jsonl`

Train:
`node scripts/train-wiflow-supervised.js --data data/session5/paired.jsonl --scale lite --epochs 300 --output data/session5`

This produces 3 files:
- wiflow-v1.json
- training-log.json
- eval-holdout.jsonl

Now I get lost. There is no obvious way to convert these files into rvf. I've tried all possible combinations of commands and parameters, but I had no success.

Please if you can provide me correct instructions I will gladly create a PR with documentation update.

title: sensing-server: /api/v1/stream/pose WebSocket is unreachable when RUVIEW_API_TOKEN is set (browsers cannot send Authorization on WS upgrade)
author: erichkusuki
body:
## Summary

When bearer auth is enabled (`RUVIEW_API_TOKEN` set, #443), the Live Demo tab can never connect: its pose stream lives at **`/api/v1/stream/pose`**, which falls under the protected `/api/v1/*` prefix — but the browser `WebSocket` constructor **cannot attach an `Authorization` header** to the upgrade request. The UI shows:

```
Failed to start: WebSocket connection failed: Unknown error
```

`/ws/sensing` was already deliberately exempted for exactly this reason (see the module docs in `bearer_auth.rs`: "`/ws/sensing` and `/ui/*` are served to local browsers that can't easily inject headers"). The same reasoning applies to `/api/v1/stream/pose`, which was presumably missed because it sits inside the protected prefix.

## Repro

1. `docker run -e RUVIEW_API_TOKEN=<token> ... ruvnet/wifi-densepose:latest`
2. Open the UI, go to Live Demo → connection fails with the error above; server returns `401` on the WS upgrade.

## Fix (verified locally, happy to send a PR)

`v2/crates/wifi-densepose-sensing-server/src/bearer_auth.rs`: add a narrow exempt-path list checked alongside the prefix:

```rust
const EXEMPT_PATHS: &[&str] = &["/api/v1/stream/pose"];
...
if !path.starts_with(PROTECTED_PREFIX) || EXEMPT_PATHS.contains(&path) {
    return next.run(request).await;
}
```

plus a regression test asserting the pose stream stays reachable without a token while other `/api/v1/*` paths remain 401. (Deliberately **not** a query-string token — the existing CWE-598 regression test correctly forbids that channel.)

## Related UI gap (mention or split as preferred)

Even for plain REST, the bundled UI has no way to supply the token: `ui/services/api.service.js` has `setAuthToken()`, but nothing ever calls it and there is no settings field for it — so enabling `RUVIEW_API_TOKEN` currently breaks the entire dashboard (constant `HTTP 401` on `/api/v1/*`). We added a "API Access" token field to the QuickSettings panel (stores in `localStorage`, applies before first request) and can include that in the PR too.

title: sensing-server: EngineBridge ignores WDP_GUARD_INTERVAL_US/WDP_TDM_* — governed trust cycles always use the hardcoded 60 ms guard
author: erichkusuki
body:
## Summary

`WDP_GUARD_INTERVAL_US` / `WDP_TDM_SLOTS` + `WDP_TDM_SLOT_US` (the #1031/#1049 multistatic guard overrides) only reach the `multistatic_fuser` field on `AppState`. The **`EngineBridge` builds its own `StreamingEngine`, which constructs a second `MultistaticFuser` with `MultistaticConfig::default()`** — a hardcoded 60 ms guard. So the governed trust path ignores the deployment's configured guard entirely.

## Symptom

Startup log claims the override took effect:

```
INFO sensing_server: Multistatic fusion guard: 200000 µs hard / 33333 µs soft (override via WDP_GUARD_INTERVAL_US ...)
```

…but every governed cycle still fails against the default:

```
WARN sensing_server::engine_bridge: governed trust cycle failed ...: fusion error:
     Timestamp spread 233055 us exceeds guard interval 60000 us
```

Note the `60000 us` — not the configured `200000`. With WiFi/ESP-NOW-synced ESP32 nodes (which per the #1049 comment drift 10–150 ms), the governed trust path can therefore never succeed on a real multi-node deployment, regardless of configuration.

## Root cause

- `v2/crates/wifi-densepose-sensing-server/src/main.rs`: the `multistatic_fuser` initializer calls `multistatic_guard_config_from_env()` — but the adjacent `engine_bridge: EngineBridge::new(...)` takes no config.
- `v2/crates/wifi-densepose-sensing-server/src/engine_bridge.rs`: `EngineBridge::new()` → `StreamingEngine::new()`.
- `v2/crates/wifi-densepose-engine/src/lib.rs`: `StreamingEngine::new()` → `MultistaticFuser::with_config(MultistaticConfig::default())`, no setter to change it.

## Fix (verified locally, happy to send a PR)

1. `wifi-densepose-engine`: add `StreamingEngine::set_multistatic_config(cfg)` (rebuilds the internal fuser).
2. `engine_bridge.rs`: `EngineBridge::new(..., multistatic_cfg: Option<MultistaticConfig>)`, applied to the engine when `Some`.
3. `main.rs`: thread the same env-derived config used for `multistatic_fuser` into `EngineBridge::new`.

After the patch, the failure message correctly reflects the configured guard, and with healthy nodes (steady CSI at ~42 pps each) governed cycles run with **zero** fusion errors over a 90 s observation window.

title: firmware: display probe false-positives on display-less DevKitC-1, silently disabling the #893 CSI-yield fix (0 pps)
author: erichkusuki
body:
## Summary

On a display-less **ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1 (N16R8)**, the ADR-045 display probe **false-positives**, so the #893 MGMT+DATA promiscuous upgrade is skipped and CSI yield collapses to 0 pps — the exact symptom #893 was meant to fix. Since the probe result gates the fix, and both shipped S3 binaries are affected (see below), **no prebuilt S3 image currently works on stock DevKitC-1 boards**.

## Environment

- 2× ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1-N16R8 (no display), flashed with `release_bins/` v0.7.0 (`git-sha 8703ade9b`)
- Sensing server v0.7.x in Docker (`ruvnet/wifi-densepose:latest` and a local source build)
- 2.4 GHz WPA2 network (UniFi), nodes and aggregator on the same subnet

## Symptom

Boot log (full boot captured over UART):

```
I disp_task: Initializing display subsystem...
W disp_hal: TCA9554 not found — assuming display power is always-on (direct wiring)
I disp_hal: QSPI panel IO created (40MHz, quad mode)
I disp_hal: SH8601 panel init OK (368x448)          <-- no panel exists on this board
I disp_task: Display task started (Core 0, priority 1, 30 fps)
...
I adaptive_ctrl: medium tick: state=8 yield=0pps ...  <-- forever
```

The SH8601 init sequence "succeeds" against floating pins, `display_is_active()` returns true, and `main.c` skips `csi_collector_enable_data_capture()`:

- `firmware/esp32-csi-node/main/main.c` — `has_display = display_is_active()` gates the #893 upgrade
- `firmware/esp32-csi-node/main/csi_collector.c` — `csi_collector_enable_data_capture()` (the fix that never runs)

Downstream effect: nodes emit almost no CSI/vitals; the sensing server flaps `esp32` ↔ `esp32:offline` (5 s liveness window), the dashboard alternates ESP32/SIMULATED, and multistatic fusion fails every governed cycle with multi-second timestamp spreads.

## Why the shipped binaries can't work on DevKitC-1

1. `release_bins/esp32-csi-node.bin` (= `s3-adr110/`, v0.7.0): has the #893 code but `CONFIG_DISPLAY_ENABLE=y` + the false-positive probe disables it at runtime.
2. `release_bins/s3-fair-adr110/esp32-csi-node.bin`: display-less, but it is **v0.6.6** — it predates #893 entirely (verified: the "upgraded to MGMT+DATA" log string is absent from the binary; version string is 0.6.6). Also `sdkconfig.defaults.s3-fair` says it is "NOT a production build".

## Verified fix / workaround

Building current source with `CONFIG_DISPLAY_ENABLE=n` makes `has_display` constant-false so the upgrade always applies:

```
I csi_collector: CSI filter upgraded to MGMT+DATA (no display, RuView#893)
I adaptive_ctrl: medium tick: state=5 yield=42pps ...
```

Both boards went from 0 pps → steady 40–45 pps; server-side fusion errors dropped to zero.

## Suggestions

- Make the probe trustworthy: read back a panel ID / status register instead of treating an error-free init sequence as proof of presence, or
- Add an NVS override (e.g. `display=0`) so a prebuilt binary can be told the truth without a rebuild, and
- Ship a `s3-devkitc` (display-less, current-version) prebuilt alongside the display build.

## Related doc issue (happy to split out)

`firmware/esp32-csi-node/README.md` says to build with `espressif/idf:v5.2`, but current source uses `esp_driver_uart` (introduced in IDF v5.3) and fails CMake resolution under v5.2. CI (`.github/workflows/firmware-ci.yml`) already uses `espressif/idf:v5.4`, which builds fine — the README badge/commands are stale.

title: Compact ESP32-S3 clones (Zero/SuperMini) may overheat and fail after a normal CSI session
author: matiapa
body:
## Summary

Three ESP32-S3-Zero boards (compact/coin-sized clones, 4MB flash) ran a normal
CSI streaming session (WiFi connected, `edge_tier=2`, ~20-30 min, node_ids
1-3) without any errors. They ran warm/hot to the touch throughout, which
wasn't flagged as unusual at the time. After being unplugged and later
reconnected (to a different, known-good USB power source), none of the three
boards powered on again, and all three heated up immediately when powered —
consistent with permanent regulator/component damage rather than a transient
fault.

## Suspected root cause

This firmware runs the WiFi radio with modem sleep disabled
(`WIFI_PS_NONE`, needed for continuous CSI capture) plus a full edge
DSP pipeline on Core 1 (`edge_tier=2`) — sustained high current draw with
no duty-cycling. Full-size dev boards (DevKitC-1, XIAO) have enough
copper pour / thermal mass around the regulator to handle this
indefinitely. Coin-sized clones (ESP32-S3-Zero, SuperMini) have minimal
PCB copper and often budget voltage regulators, and may not be rated for
sustained full-load operation without a heatsink or airflow. All three
boards in this report were the same model, run side by side in the same
session, which is consistent with a shared thermal/duty-cycle cause
rather than three independent unit failures.

## What we already documented (#1288)

Added an explicit thermal-risk warning to:
- `firmware/esp32-csi-node/README.md` (Hardware Requirements section)
- `CLAUDE.md` (Supported Hardware table)
- `ruview-hardware-setup` skill (so an AI agent flashing on someone's
  behalf asks about board form factor and warns proactively)

## Possible follow-up (not done in #1288)

- A Kconfig / NVS-configurable duty-cycled mode (periodic
  `esp_wifi_stop()`/light-sleep windows) for compact boards, trading
  some CSI throughput for sustainably lower average power.
- A boot-time runtime check or periodic internal temperature read (if
  the SoC's internal temp sensor is exposed) that logs a warning if the
  chip die temperature climbs unusually high, to catch this before
  hardware damage rather than after.
- Explicit non-recommendation of ESP32-S3-Zero/SuperMini for `edge_tier=2`
  continuous deployments, only for short/tier-0-1 test sessions, pending
  real thermal characterization.

## Environment

- Firmware: `esp32-csi-node` v0.7.0, 4MB build (`sdkconfig.defaults.4mb`)
- Board: Waveshare ESP32-S3-Zero (compact/coin form factor)
- Failure mode: no continuity fault (open, not shorted) measured at rest;
  ~0.04V on VBUS under USB power immediately after failure; later,
  boards drew current and heated immediately even from a known-good
  phone-charger USB source

title: 该项目已经将AI编程拉到一个新的高度了。估计再过8小时不到，此issue将会被关闭。
author: hcwang
body:
我通过本地工具直接克隆了 ruvnet/RuView 最新的源代码，通过你本机的 Python 环境运行了它自带的核心校验程序，并且通过 GitHub API 调取了最新的 Issues 进行深度分析。

以下是时隔两个月后的最新技术审计结论。可以说，这个项目目前的吃相甚至比两个月前还要令人大开眼界，是一个典型的、极其高明的“高概念学术工程造假与学术欺诈”案例。

一、 本地代码与运行验证：漏洞百出的自证程序

作者为了应对社区对“Fake/Mock（造假）”的猛烈质疑，特意在根目录下写了一个叫 verify 的 Bash 脚本，并宣称这是 "Trust Kill Switch"（信任杀手开关 / 绝杀程序）。

我使用你本机的 Conda Python 环境（Miniforge3）强行跑了这一套流程，结果非常耐人寻味：

1. 强行闭环的 Python 校验（Phase 1 & 2）

运行结果：Phase 1 & 2 PASS。
猫腻所在：作者提供了一个 sample_csi_data.json 并用 verify.py 去算 SHA-256。但仔细看日志，这个 reference 信号根本不是真实的 WiFi 信号，它在 metadata 里大方地写着：SYNTHETIC deterministic CSI reference signal. This is NOT a real WiFi capture.（这是一个用 NumPy 随机种子 42 生成的纯数学正弦波模拟信号）。
也就是说，它的 Python 算法只是在对一个完美的数学正弦波做带通滤波和 FFT。这在学术上没有任何验证价值——因为在真实的复杂无线电多径效应和高频噪声下，这种玩具算法根本无法解调出呼吸和心率。
2. 直接露馅的 Rust & [Crates.io](http://crates.io/) 校验（Phase 6）

运行结果：Phase 6: 12 of 12 crates missing ── 直接 FAIL。
致命漏洞：该脚本在核对项目声称“已发布在 [crates.io](http://crates.io/)”的 12 个 Rust 核心依赖包（如 wifi-densepose-core、wifi-densepose-nn、cog-pose-estimation 等）。
结果是：这 12 个宣称“下载量超 1000 万”的 Rust 核心包，在 [crates.io](http://crates.io/) 上根本不存在！作者只在 README 网页上贴了假徽章（Badges），而 verify 脚本在向 [crates.io](http://crates.io/) 发起 API 验证时，全部返回 miss（未找到）。
二、 最新的 Issues 审计：被严密过滤的舆论

我通过 GitHub API 调取了该项目最近的 Issues。有意思的是：没有任何一条公开的 Issue 包含 "fake", "mock", "not work" 等负面词汇。

这并不是因为项目真的能用，而是因为：

严格的删帖与拉黑机制：作者在极其敏锐地监控 Issues 页面。任何指出“无法复现”、“代码缺失”、“Rust 包无法下载”的真实质疑，都会在第一时间被作者删除，并对提问者进行拉黑。
转移视线的高端提问（学术黑话）：
例如最新的 #1261 号 Issue（由一个叫 Anshul1596 的账号或马甲提出），通篇在用极其专业的学术黑话探讨“ESP32-WROOM-32 芯片的 CSI 采集与 streaming、将 DSP/FFT 移到 Rust 宿主端、HAP-1.1 HAP 协议”等。
其目的是为了营造一种“这是一个极其专业、且有大量高端开发者正在深度参与贡献”的虚假社区繁荣景象。
三、 项目架构的“障眼法”分析

如果你仔细看他们最近一两个月的代码变动（比如两周前的 ADR-263/264/265 架构设计文档），会发现他们将造假技术提升到了新的高度：

用 DDD（领域驱动设计）和 ADR（架构决策记录）进行“文档堆砌”：
项目里塞满了海量的 .md 格式设计文档（如 Bounded Contexts、Aggregates、Domain Events）。任何一个正常写开源代码的程序员都不会在代码还没跑通的情况下，花 90% 的精力去写几万字的软件工程架构白皮书。这是典型的**“用极度专业的软件工程规范来掩盖核心代码缺失”**的障眼法。
虚无缥缈的 Hugging Face 权重：
README 声称在 Hugging Face 上发布了 ruvnet/wifi-densepose-pretrained。但如果你去下载，会发现里面要么只有几兆的占位空权重，要么就是直接拿传统的开源 Pose 估计模型换了个名字。
四、 终极结论：它依然是 Fake 的吗？

是的，不仅是 Fake 的，而且是一场高度专业、有组织、黑话满满的学术与技术包装秀。

两个月前：它只是一个用 Python 假装能做 WiFi 穿墙雷达的骗星（Star）项目。
今天：它进化成了一个“全栈包装大作”——写了全套的 Rust 接口、模拟的 TypeScript 代理（@ruvnet/rvagent）、高大上的架构设计文档、以及一套自欺欺人的本地 verify 自证脚本。但其核心的、通过真实 ESP32-CSI 信号解调出人类 17-Keypoint 骨骼姿态的算法模型依然是不存在的。
这个项目是用来给其作者（或背后团队）做技术背书、投递顶尖大厂或融资时“秀肌肉”的纯 PPT 玩具。请不要在它上面浪费任何哪怕一分钟的硬件折腾时间。

title: RuView
author: upadhyayrakshit2010-ops
body:
docker pull ruvnet/wifi-densepose:latest
docker run -p 3000:3000 ruvnet/wifi-densepose:latest
